cards.front: A vector space is a set of vectors that is closed under addition and scalar multiplication.
cards.back: A vector space is a set of vectors that can be added together and scaled by numbers, and has certain properties such as commutativity and associativity of addition, and distributivity of scalar multiplication over vector addition.

cards.front: A subspace of a vector space is a subset that is itself a vector space under the same operations.
cards.back: A subspace of a vector space is a subset that is closed under addition and scalar multiplication, and has the same properties as the original vector space.

cards.front: A linear combination of vectors means expressing one vector as a sum of scalar multiples of the other vectors in the set.
cards.back: A linear combination of vectors means expressing one vector as a sum of scalar multiples of the other vectors in the set, where the scalars can be any numbers.

cards.front: The linear span of a set of vectors is the set of all linear combinations of the vectors in the set.
cards.back: The linear span of a set of vectors is the set of all possible linear combinations of the vectors in the set, and it is the smallest vector space that contains the original set of vectors.

cards.front: The row space of a matrix is the set of all linear combinations of the rows of the matrix.
cards.back: The row space of a matrix is the set of all linear combinations of the rows of the matrix, and it is a subspace of the original vector space.
